message:
fix(fr-conformance): share ACL/keyspace across AUTH-dedicated sessions + ACL LOG canonicalizer — closes br-frankenredis-faqe

The last 11 ACL live-oracle XFAILs were all rooted in a harness
quirk: connection-mode commands (AUTH/HELLO/SELECT/…) rebuilt a
brand-new Runtime per case so later cases wouldn't inherit the
mode, but the fresh Runtime also lost the ACL subsystem state
that prior SETUSER cases had installed on the shared runtime.
Upstream suffered no such loss — its persistent server keeps the
ACL subsystem alive across fresh TCP connections.

- fr-runtime: `Runtime::with_isolated_session(|rt| …)` temporarily
  swaps the ClientSession for `ClientSession::default()` and
  restores it on return. Server state (ACL, keyspace, pubsub, …)
  stays shared.
- fr-conformance: dedicated-connection path now calls
  `with_isolated_session` on the main runtime instead of spinning
  up an isolated Runtime. Both runtimes share the ACL subsystem,
  matching upstream's semantics.
- fr-conformance: new `live_oracle_acl_log_drift_matches`
  canonicalizer folds the volatile fields in `ACL LOG` entries
  (age-seconds, client-info, entry-id, timestamp-created,
  timestamp-last-updated) so the stable reason/object/context/
  username/count fields drive the comparison.
- drop the 11 residual AUTH/ACL-LOG XFAILs — live oracle for
  core_acl is now 130/130 under STRICT mode.

fr-runtime 275 passed; fr-command 639 passed;
fr-conformance 180 passed, 0 failed; live_redis_core_acl 130/130
passed under FR_CONFORMANCE_LIVE_ORACLE_STRICT=1.

Closes br-frankenredis-faqe.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
